sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, I can see the white diamond is now inside the orange outline at (49, 40). The chain appears to be:
- O (14, 37) → G (25, 40) → p (32, 40) → W (49, 40) [inside orange outline]
The white diamond is now at the orange outline position. According to the Level 1 solution, after moving the head to the outline, I need to click on the tail to reverse the chain. But in Level 1, after clicking the outline, the head moved to the outline, then clicking the tail reversed the chain, and then clicking an outline pixel completed the level.
Wait, but in Level 1, the sequence was:
1. Click purple outline → head moves to outline
2. Click tail (gray diamond) → chain reverses (head at tail, tail at outline)
3. Click outline pixel → level completes
For Level 2, I've done:
1. Click orange square → white diamond disappeared, purple square moved to top chain
2. Click purple square → white diamond reappeared at (25, 40)
3. Click gray diamond → white diamond moved to (40, 42)
4. Click orange outline → white diamond moved to (49, 40)
Now the white diamond is at the orange outline. The next step might be to click on the tail of the chain to reverse it. The tail is the gray diamond at (25, 40) or the purple square at (32, 40).
Wait, looking at the current chain:
- O (14, 37) → G (25, 40) → p (32, 40) → W (49, 40)
The tail is the white diamond at (49, 40), and the head is the orange square at (14, 37). If I need to reverse the chain, I should click on the head (orange square) or the tail (white diamond).
But wait, in Level 1, after clicking the outline, the head moved to the outline. Then clicking the tail (gray diamond) reversed the chain. So the tail was the object at the end of the chain opposite the head.
In Level 2, the chain is:
- O (14, 37) → G (25, 40) → p (32, 40) → W (49, 40)
The head is O (14, 37) and the tail is W (49, 40). But the white diamond is the head, not the tail. Wait, I'm confused about which end is the head and which is the tail.
Let me think about this differently. In Level 1:
- The white diamond was the head of the chain.
- Clicking on the outline moved the white diamond to the outline.
- Clicking on the tail (gray diamond) reversed the chain.
- Clicking on the outline pixel completed the level.
In Level 2:
- The white diamond is now at the orange outline (49, 40).
- The chain is: O (14, 37) → G (25, 40) → p (32, 40) → W (49, 40).
- If I click on the tail (which is the orange square at (14, 37)?), the chain might reverse.
But wait, in Level 1, the tail was the gray diamond at the end of the chain opposite the white diamond. In Level 2, the tail would be the orange square at (14, 37), which is the other end of the chain.
Let me try clicking on the orange square at (14, 37) to see if it reverses the chain.
